summary:
Necrosis drives acute inflammation by releasing DAMPs such as HMGB1 and ATP, which trigger innate immune responses and manifest clinically as redness, heat, swelling, pain, and tissue dysfunction—the classic phases of acute inflammation. Among listed options, it uniquely represents the fundamental mechanism underlying these observable stages more broadly than apoptotic or programmed cell-death alternatives.  

\boxed{D}

\boxed{D}. Answer choice: B
